sections.title: Recherche de base
sections.html:
(machine translation pending) To search, type your query into the search box and press Enter (or click the Search button).

Multiple words are combined with AND by default. For example, open source finds documents containing both "open" and "source".

sections.title: Opérateurs de recherche
sections.html:
(machine translation pending)

OperatorDescriptionExample
AND / &&Both terms must appearfess AND search
OR / ||Either term may appearfess OR opensearch
NOT / -Exclude the termfess -lucene
"..."Exact phrase"full text search"
site:Restrict to a sitesite:example.com fess
filetype:Restrict to a file typefiletype:pdf fess

sections.title: Caractères génériques
sections.html:
(machine translation pending) You can use * to match any sequence of characters and ? to match a single character.

Examples: fess* matches "fess", "fessbook", "fessful". te?t matches "test" or "text".

sections.title: Requêtes par plage
sections.html: (machine translation pending) Use square brackets to search for a range of values: content_length:[1024 TO 10240] finds documents with sizes between 1KB and 10KB.

sections.title: Filtres par facettes
sections.html: (machine translation pending) Use the sidebar facets to narrow your search by label, time period, file size, or file type. Click a facet item to apply it, and click the chip at the top of the results to remove it.

sections.title: Raccourcis clavier
sections.html:
(machine translation pending)

  • / — Focus the search box
  • Esc — Close suggestions or modal
  • Enter — Run search or select suggestion

sections.title: Recherche par champ
sections.html:
(machine translation pending) You can search any field by typing the field name followed by a colon : and then the term you are looking for. For example, to find documents with "Fess" in the title:

title:Fess

The available fields include url, host, site, title, content, content_length, last_modified, and mimetype. These are customizable.

sections.title: Tri
sections.html:
(machine translation pending) The sort: operator sorts results by a specified field. The format is sort:<field>.<order>, where <order> is asc or desc. For example, to search for "Fess" sorted by content length descending:

Fess sort:content_length.desc

Available sort fields include created, content_length, and last_modified. These are customizable.

sections.title: Pondération (Boost)
sections.html:
(machine translation pending) To boost a term, use the ^ symbol followed by a boost factor (a number) at the end of the search term.

Fess^100

sections.title: Recherche approximative
sections.html:
(machine translation pending) To perform a fuzzy search, use the ~ symbol at the end of a single word. The optional number after ~ (0 to 1) controls the similarity threshold. The default is 0.5.

Fess~0.5
